summary: Brazilian modernist architect (1907–2012), Pritzker Prize winner, and the primary designer of Brasilia — Brazil's planned capital. Worked closely with urban planner Lucio Costa, and contributed to the UN Headquarters design with Le Corbusier. Known for his use of curved reinforced concrete, hyperboloid arches, and parabolic vaults. Key works include the Cathedral of Brasilia, the National Congress of Brazil, the Church of Saint Francis of Assisi (Pampulha), the Niteroi Contemporary Art Museum, the Copan Building, and the French Communist Party Headquarters.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Oscar Niemeyer (full name: Oscar Ribeiro de Almeida Niemeyer Soares Filho) was a Brazilian modernist architect, Pritzker Prize winner, and the chief designer behind Brasilia. He collaborated with urban planner Lucio Costa on the capital project, with Costa handling layout and Niemeyer designing the buildings. The city's buildings lie along the Monumental Axis of an airplane-shaped city plan. Niemeyer also contributed to the design of the United Nations Headquarters in New York (based on a joint submission with Le Corbusier; Niemeyer submitted Plan 32) — the two architects reportedly feuded during that collaboration. He went into exile in Paris and designed the headquarters of the French Communist Party (with a massive underground 'Worker's Hall'). He teamed up with Converse in 2012 to release Chuck Taylor trainers printed with a poem about his longing for the 'free and sensual curve.' He designed the 2003 Serpentine Pavilion in London, and created the 'Alta' lounge chair in collaboration with his daughter. Wikipedia

works.name: Cathedral of Brasilia
works.description: The Metropolitan Cathedral of Our Lady Aparecida (Cathedral of Brasilia), one of Niemeyer's most iconic works. Built from sixteen hyperbolic (hyperboloid) concrete columns that curve outward and support a glass roof. Often described as crown-shaped. Three angels are suspended by steel cables inside. Partially cooled by a reflecting pool on its roof. The cathedral is dedicated to Our Lady of Aparecida, patroness of Brazil, and is a key landmark along Brasilia's Monumental Axis. Wikipedia

works.name: National Congress of Brazil
works.description: Niemeyer's design for the National Congress of Brazil in Brasilia, situated along the Monumental Axis. The building is topped by two white domes — one facing up and one facing down (or described as a bowl and a dome) — symbolizing the bicameral legislature (Senate and Chamber of Deputies). Landscape architect Roberto Burle Marx designed the surrounding gardens and landscapes outside the building. Wikipedia

works.name: Church of Saint Francis of Assisi (Pampulha)
works.description: Part of Niemeyer's Pampulha Project — a suburb complex built around a man-made lagoon in Belo Horizonte (Pampulha). The church is made of four concrete parabolas and an inverted obelisk. The exterior features blue mosaics and murals by artist Candido Portinari (who did quasi-cubist work), mostly depicting Saint Francis of Assisi. Its hanger-inspired style scandalized the clergy and delayed use by a decade; the local bishop reportedly called it 'the devil's bomb shelter.' A copy of Picasso's Guernica appears inside as a tapestry. The centerpiece of the broader Pampulha complex was a casino (not the church itself). Wikipedia

works.name: Niteroi Contemporary Art Museum
works.description: A contemporary art museum in Niteroi, Brazil, designed by Niemeyer. Saucer-shaped (often likened to a UFO or flying saucer) and set on a cliffside above the water, surrounded entirely by a reflecting pool. A winding red-carpeted ramp leads up to a white cupola. Sometimes described as 'white-concrete.' Wikipedia

works.name: Copan Building (Edificio Copan)
works.description: A large residential skyscraper in São Paulo designed by Niemeyer. Known for its distinctive S-shaped (sinuous) facade, made up of 72 million exterior tiles. Currently under renovation due to tiles falling off. Located on the Rua Barao de Itapetininga in the Triangulo district of São Paulo. Wikipedia

works.name: Oscar Niemeyer Museum
works.description: Niemeyer's self-designed namesake museum, located in Curitiba, Brazil (capital of Parana state). Features a distinctive wide eye-shaped tower (nicknamed 'the Eye') placed atop a single yellow pillar (blue glass annex on yellow rectangular tiled support). Wikipedia

comprehensive_summary:
Oscar Niemeyer (full name Oscar Ribeiro de Almeida Niemeyer Soares Filho) was a Brazilian modernist architect and Pritzker Prize winner (awarded in 1988, jointly), most famous as the chief architect of Brasilia — Brazil's planned capital city. His collaboration with urban planner Lucio Costa defined Brasilia: Costa designed the airplane-shaped city layout (also described as bird-shaped), with buildings arranged along a central Monumental Axis, while Niemeyer designed the principal structures. The project was commissioned by President Juscelino Kubitschek, who famously promised 'fifty years of progress in five.' Niemeyer's work is characterized by bold uses of curved reinforced concrete, hyperboloid forms, and parabolic vaults.

The Cathedral of Brasilia (Metropolitan Cathedral of Our Lady of Aparecida — patroness of Brazil) is among his most celebrated works, built from sixteen hyperbolic concrete columns that curve outward and support a glass roof in a crown-like form. Three angels (or angel statues) are suspended by steel cables inside. The cathedral is partially cooled by a reflecting pool on its roof. The National Congress of Brazil is another Brasilia landmark, topped by two white domes — one facing up and one facing down (a bowl and a dome) — symbolizing the bicameral legislature (Senate and Chamber of Deputies). Landscape architect Roberto Burle Marx designed the surrounding gardens and landscapes outside the Congress building. Both buildings lie along the Monumental Axis.

Niemeyer's earlier Pampulha Project (in Belo Horizonte) established his international reputation. The broader Pampulha complex was centered on a casino; the Church of Saint Francis of Assisi there is made of four concrete parabolas and an inverted obelisk, with blue mosaic walls and exterior murals by Candido Portinari depicting the saint — work that scandalized the clergy and delayed the church's use for a decade; the local bishop reportedly called it 'the devil's bomb shelter.' A tapestry copy of Picasso's Guernica is displayed inside.

The Niteroi Contemporary Art Museum, set on a cliffside in Niteroi, is saucer-shaped (frequently compared to a UFO or flying saucer), surrounded by a reflecting pool, and reached by a winding red-carpeted ramp leading up to a white cupola. The Copan Building (Edificio Copan), a sprawling residential tower in São Paulo located on the Rua Barao de Itapetininga in the Triangulo district, features a distinctive S-shaped facade made of 72 million exterior tiles, now under renovation due to the tiles falling off. The Oscar Niemeyer Museum in Curitiba (self-designed, in the capital of Parana state) features a wide eye-shaped structure (nicknamed 'the Eye') perched atop a yellow rectangular tiled pillar.

During political exile in Paris, Niemeyer designed the French Communist Party Headquarters, featuring a massive underground 'Worker's Hall.' He also contributed to the United Nations Headquarters in New York through a joint submission (Plan 23/32) with Le Corbusier — the two reportedly feuded during the collaboration. Other works include 'The Volcano' cultural center in Le Havre, France; the University of Haifa (all buildings in one integrated complex); a building at the University of Constantine, Algeria (Hall of the Zouaghi Slimane Geography and Earth Sciences, co-designed with Rashid Hassaine); a memorial with a 'Hall of Acts' and 'Creativity Pavilion' whose main portion depicts a hand dripping blood in a map shape; the Brasilia Palace hotel; and the 2003 Serpentine Pavilion in London. He also designed the 'Alta' lounge chair in collaboration with his daughter. In 2012, he collaborated with Converse to release Chuck Taylor trainers printed with a poem about his longing for the 'free and sensual curve.'
